proposed_from.topic: The least-favorable family of Rosenbaum sensitivity analysis (bridge: matched-study Gamma-sensitivity x Huber-Strassen minimax robust testing): for matched pairs with the alternative separated from the Gamma-null polytope (Q_i(n_i)/Q_i(1) > Gamma_i), the all-tests worst-case power frontier equals the NP frontier of the single extreme-tilt least-favorable pair; for matched sets of size >= 3 the single pair provably fails at high levels (verified level-dependent LF switch, extreme-tilt calibration invalid at alpha=0.7) and is replaced by a level-indexed least-favorable MIXTURE family supported on Q-monotone Gamma-tilts; deliverables: the Rosenbaum validity region A (levels where classical extreme-tilt calibration is minimax-sharp among ALL tests), the design-sensitivity envelope Gamma-tilde* on the common-Gamma ray (ceiling of the statistic-choice program, McNemar-consistent on pairs), and the two-sided strict-improvement region over the Bonferroni-doubled practice (verified strict gap 0.158 at Gamma=2, exact equality at Gamma=1); consumers sensitivitymv/senfm and two-sided sensitivity reporting
proposed_from.novelty_justification: The proposal is positioned as a finite conditional all-tests saddle-point question, not as a refinement of a selected-statistic sensitivity calculation. Rosenbaum1988SensitivityMultipleControls is the direct finite multiple-control comparator, but its sharp calculation is for a selected generalized signed-rank family. The closest published adversarial formulation, CohenOlsonFogarty2020AdversarialGame, optimizes only the coherent linear class built from prespecified test statistics and provides an asymptotic null law. The present open question instead ranges over all conditional rejection rules and asks for the least-favorable mixture and its support-switch inequalities. Rosenbaum1987SensitivityPermutation, GastwirthKriegerRosenbaum2000Separability, and Fogarty2025Tilted remain informed-by comparators because each fixes a statistic or procedure family.
seed_list: Level-indexed least-favorable mixtures characterize the all-tests Rosenbaum power frontier in matched sets with multiple controls.
A single extreme tilt is a uniform least-favorable null for every pair-matched test level under directional separation.
An all-tests design-sensitivity envelope gives the ceiling over score choice on the common-\(\Gamma\) ray.
A joint two-sided robust test exploits the common assignment law and can improve on doubled one-sided sensitivity bounds.
The Rosenbaum validity region is an exact divergence frontier between classical extreme-tilt calibration and a level-dependent mixture.
A sensitivity-value limit law follows from the level-indexed least-favorable mixture rather than a selected signed score.
A critical hidden-bias threshold identifies when joint two-sided calibration ceases to dominate Bonferroni doubling.
Permutation inversion of the robust likelihood-ratio test yields finite-sample sensitivity confidence sets.

seed_details.one_line: Level-indexed least-favorable mixtures characterize the all-tests Rosenbaum power frontier in matched sets with multiple controls.

anchor_paper.author: Gastwirth, Krieger, and Rosenbaum
anchor_paper.claim: The multiple-control optimization is generally nonseparable and becomes asymptotically separable for specified rank procedures.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The object is a capacity-mixture saddle point, not an LP restatement of a selected-statistic tail bound.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from finite linear-program duality.
seed_details.rebuttal: Duality yields some mixture, but not the directional \(Q\)-monotone support, the Neyman--Pearson saddle, or the collapse region for the classical tilt.
seed_details.burned_reason: The finite all-tests LP/oracle-boundary package is sound at subfield tier but exhausted as a field-level headline; reuse it only as substrate for a genuinely broader implementable theorem.

seed_details.one_line: A single extreme tilt is a uniform least-favorable null for every pair-matched test level under directional separation.

anchor_paper.author: Rosenbaum
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp \(\Gamma\)-bounds are obtained for named permutation procedures.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: This is an all-tests representative-pair theorem, not a relabeling of a signed-score bound.
seed_details.referee_objection: The pair result is immediate by monotone likelihood ratios.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is retained as a necessary boundary theorem and sanity reduction, not as the standalone headline.

seed_details.one_line: An all-tests design-sensitivity envelope gives the ceiling over score choice on the common-\(\Gamma\) ray.

anchor_paper.author: Rosenbaum
anchor_paper.claim: Design sensitivity measures asymptotic separation from hidden bias.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The focal object is an efficiency-style envelope rather than another selected-score comparison.
seed_details.referee_objection: Without the finite least-favorable law, the proposed ceiling is circular.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed depends on the mixture kernel and is deferred as a consumer rather than combined with it.

seed_details.one_line: A joint two-sided robust test exploits the common assignment law and can improve on doubled one-sided sensitivity bounds.

anchor_paper.author: Zhao
anchor_paper.claim: A conventional two-sided sensitivity value doubles the smaller one-sided upper bound.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The mathematical object is one joint rejection region rather than a multiplicity correction.
seed_details.referee_objection: A two-sided test is merely a union-bound refinement.
seed_details.rebuttal: The directional worst cases are coupled by one assignment law, but exact strictness requires a separate composite-alternative game.

seed_details.one_line: The Rosenbaum validity region is an exact divergence frontier between classical extreme-tilt calibration and a level-dependent mixture.

anchor_paper.author: Huber and Strassen
anchor_paper.claim: A fixed representative pair is minimax under an order-two capacity condition.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The frontier is derived from saddle inequalities, not defined as an optimizer label.
seed_details.referee_objection: The region is tautological if defined by validity.
seed_details.rebuttal: It survives only within the mixture seed, which asks for primitive necessary-and-sufficient inequalities and support switches.

seed_details.one_line: A sensitivity-value limit law follows from the level-indexed least-favorable mixture rather than a selected signed score.

anchor_paper.author: Zhao
anchor_paper.claim: Pair-matched sensitivity values are asymptotically normal.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: This is a limit-law seed, not a bare estimation rung.
seed_details.referee_objection: The non-smooth support switches make a universal central limit theorem implausible.
seed_details.rebuttal: A credible theorem would need a margin away from switch levels; no such primitive regime was located in the scan.
seed_details.status: dropped after literature and feasibility scan

seed_details.one_line: A critical hidden-bias threshold identifies when joint two-sided calibration ceases to dominate Bonferroni doubling.

anchor_paper.author: Zhao
anchor_paper.claim: Two-sided reporting doubles the smaller separately bounded tail.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The target is a critical \(\Gamma\), not qualitative sensitivity.
seed_details.referee_objection: The threshold depends on a fully specified alternative and offers no robust general theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: A useful result requires a composite two-sided alternative class that is not yet pinned down.

seed_details.one_line: Permutation inversion of the robust likelihood-ratio test yields finite-sample sensitivity confidence sets.

anchor_paper.author: Rosenbaum
anchor_paper.claim: Sensitivity tests can be inverted for inferential procedures after a statistic is chosen.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: The construction is exact inversion under assignment symmetry.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a routine inversion once the test exists.
seed_details.rebuttal: The objection lands; it is a downstream consumer, not an independent proposal kernel.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Rosenbaum1987SensitivityPermutation defines the bounded-odds model and proves sharp sensitivity bounds after selecting a permutation statistic.
HuberStrassen1973Minimax identifies the capacity condition under which one representative null--alternative pair solves an all-tests robust-testing problem.
GastwirthKriegerRosenbaum2000Separability states that multiple-control optimization is generally nonseparable, then proves asymptotic separability for selected rank procedures.
Rosenbaum2004DesignSensitivity turns robustness to hidden bias into an asymptotic separation threshold, but its comparisons are within chosen procedures.
Zhao2019SensitivityValue proves pair-matched sensitivity-value theory, reports doubled one-sided two-sided calibration, and explicitly leaves a simple multiple-control formula open.
Fogarty2020Adversarial optimizes coherent one-sided evidence in a different adversarial sensitivity game.
Fogarty2025Tilted gives a closed-form limiting law and design sensitivity for a candidate tilted statistic with multiple controls; its introduction notes that conventional multiple-control worst-case patterns lack a closed form and can change with \(\Gamma\).
The settled result is score-specific sharpness or candidate-statistic limit theory. The unresolved object is the least-favorable law for the game over all tests, including whether it is a single extreme tilt or a level-indexed mixture.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: pair theorem and matched-set mixture question
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The claims condition on fixed Gamma, a uniform product alternative assignment baseline, and specified dominated independent alternative record laws.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any exact least-favorable-law statement applies only to this conditional Fisher-sharp-null testing game, not weak-null inference, ATE estimation, two-sided testing, or design-sensitivity asymptotics.

banked_reason: Math PASS, but the finite LP diagnostic and fixed-record Q-known oracle boundary remain below the requested field novelty floor after directed revision and independent validity gating.
banked_proposal_promise_gap: The field-tier promise requires an implementable level-indexed least-favorable procedure and the exact joint two-sided improvement region, not only a finite diagnostic and Q-known oracle ceiling.

seeds_burned.one_liner: Level-indexed least-favorable mixtures characterize the all-tests Rosenbaum power frontier in matched sets with multiple controls.
seeds_burned.reason: The finite all-tests LP/oracle-boundary package is sound at subfield tier but exhausted as a field-level headline; reuse it only as substrate for a genuinely broader implementable theorem.
